data.narrative:
eleQtron is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Christof Wunderlich, Michael Johanning, and Jan Henrik Leisse, having raised €50M+. The valuation exercise draws on a comp universe of 3 public and 8 private peers in the quantum computing sector, including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion among private players, and IONQ, RGTI, and QBTS in public markets.

Peer-median valuation methodology was not applicable to this analysis. The quantum computing sector contains only 5 same-sector peers overall, and none satisfied the filtering criteria for sufficient comparable depth—zero datapoints emerged at the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. A reliance on all-sector median would introduce material bias given the stage and sector heterogeneity. This constraint reflects the thinly-traded and early-stage nature of the quantum computing venture landscape.

Without a triangulated result from traditional comps analysis, the valuation of eleQtron remains undetermined by this exercise. Key uncertainties preventing quantitative synthesis include the absence of sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with disclosed valuations, the wide dispersion in quantum computing business models and technology approaches, and limited public market trading depth for comparable reference. A credible range would require either proprietary access to recent private fundraising data for narrower peer cohorts or methodological expansion to venture-backed round pricing in the quantum sector.
